title: Beating the Weather
concept: Mitigating environmental variations involves specialized augmentations and pixel normalization techniques.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What makes weather distortions like fog hard for models?

options.text: They drop contrast, smoothing the edge gradients that CNN filters look for
They increase the memory footprint of the weights
They save the images in unreadable formats

exercises.explain: Weather limits visibility and contrast, blending edge details and confusing localized filters.

cards.title: Virtual Storms in Code
cards.body: If you don't have the budget to travel the world chasing rainstorms, you can build them inside your computer. By using math to inject synthetic raindrops, fog haze, and snow blobs into sunny training photos, we teach the model to see through the storm.

cards.title: Weather Augmentation
cards.body: Weather Augmentations are mathematical filters applied to training images to simulate physical conditions. They include: dehazing algorithms, contrast adjustment (CLAHE), and synthetic weather noise overlay.

cards.title: Fog Goggles
cards.body: Dehazing algorithms are like wearing polarized fog goggles. They calculate how much light was scattered across the image and subtract that grey haze, restoring contrast and bringing out the hidden contours of objects.

cards.title: Contrast Normalization (CLAHE)
cards.body: Engineers apply **CLAHE** (Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization). Unlike global contrast adjustments, CLAHE enhances contrast in local tiles, revealing edges in foggy or dark corners without overexposing bright spots.

cards.title: Augmentation is not a magic fix
cards.body: You might think adding 100% synthetic weather solves all production shifts. But if your synthetic rain looks like fake static lines, the model will learn to ignore those specific lines, failing when faced with thick real-world rain droplets.

cards.title: Dehazing Results
cards.body: How preprocessing dehazing algorithms restore edge details:

left.text:
Low contrast, washed out.
Edge gradients are extremely weak.
CNN misses key shapes.

right.text:
Haze subtracted mathematically.
Contrast and contours restored.
CNN detects objects clearly.

exercises.prompt: What is CLAHE in image preprocessing?
exercises.explain: CLAHE stands for Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization, boosting local contrast to reveal hidden edges.

options.text: An adaptive histogram equalization technique that boosts contrast locally in small image regions
A convolutional layer that compresses image files to save GPU memory
An annotation conversion tool that translates XML to JSON
A model pruning strategy that deletes small weights

exercises.prompt: True or False: Synthetic weather augmentations should look as physically realistic as possible to help the model generalize to real storms.
exercises.explain: True. Highly realistic simulations ensure the features learned translate directly to real-world deployment.
Dehazing algorithms mathematically remove fog haze, restoring local contrast gradients.
exercises.sentence: Algorithms that estimate light scattering to remove visual fog are called ___ algorithms.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is a software strategy to mitigate weather shifts?
exercises.explain: Synthetic weather overlays during training train the model's weights to be robust to those visual patterns.

options.text: Adding synthetic rain streaks and fog overlays to training images during augmentation
Wiping the physical camera lens with a cloth manually
Increasing the batch size during the validation phase

exercises.prompt: You are deploying a harbor traffic camera. In thick fog, standard detectors miss 50% of boats. What is the most cost-effective software fix?

options.text: Add a dehazing preprocessing step in your inference pipeline, and retrain the detector with synthetic fog augmentations.
Switch to a larger, heavier backbone architecture without modifying the inputs.
Replace the camera with a microphone to listen for boat horns.
Only evaluate boat traffic on clear, sunny days.

exercises.explain: Dehazing restores the visual input quality, while fog augmentations train the model to handle residual low-contrast features. Next up: Sensor Fusion!
